report_name: New 'Icarus' Extortion Group Hits Klue, Steals Customer Salesforce Data via OAuth Attack

threat_actors.name: Icarus
threat_actors.description: A newly identified extortion group that compromised the Klue platform to steal OAuth tokens, which were then used to access and exfiltrate customer data from integrated Salesforce environments.
threat_actors.goals: Extortion via theft of sensitive customer CRM data.

attack_patterns.name: Steal Application Access Token
attack_patterns.description: The Icarus group located and exfiltrated stored OAuth tokens from Klue's production environment. These tokens were used by Klue to interact with its customers' Salesforce instances.

attack_patterns.name: Cloud Accounts
attack_patterns.description: Attackers used stolen OAuth tokens to authenticate to the Salesforce API, bypassing traditional user authentication like MFA. From Salesforce's perspective, these were valid sessions initiated by the authorized Klue application.

attack_patterns.name: Data from Cloud Storage
attack_patterns.description: Using authenticated API access from the stolen OAuth tokens, the Icarus group queried the Salesforce environments of Klue's customers to exfiltrate sensitive CRM data.

identities.name: Klue
identities.description: A market intelligence platform whose systems were compromised by the Icarus group, leading to the theft of OAuth tokens that provided access to its customers' Salesforce data.

identities.name: Salesforce
identities.description: The CRM platform from which customer data was exfiltrated. The attackers gained access by abusing stolen OAuth tokens from the Klue application integration.

identities.name: Klue Customers
identities.description: The ultimate victims of the breach, whose sensitive CRM data stored in Salesforce was accessed and exfiltrated by the Icarus group.

indicators.name: Anomalous Salesforce API Query Activity
indicators.pattern:
title: Anomalous Salesforce API Query Activity
id: 4f5b9c1a-2d8e-4f7b-8c1d-9e8f7a6b5c4d
status: experimental
description: |
  Detects access to the Salesforce API query endpoint. While common for integrations, this activity should be monitored for anomalous patterns, such as high volume or access from unexpected sources, which could indicate data exfiltration as seen in the Icarus attack on Klue.
references:
  - https://www.bleepingcomputer.com/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-06-20
tags:
  - attack.t1530
  - attack.collection
level: medium
logsource:
  product: salesforce
  service: api
detection:
  selection:
    event_type: 'ApiEvent'
    endpoint|contains: '/query'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects access to the Salesforce API query endpoint, which could indicate data exfiltration if volumes are anomalous.

indicators.name: Unplanned Salesforce OAuth Token Revocation
indicators.pattern:
title: Unplanned Salesforce OAuth Token Revocation
id: 8a1b2c3d-4e5f-4a9b-8c1d-2e3f4a5b6c7d
status: experimental
description: |
  Detects the revocation of an OAuth token in Salesforce. While this can be a legitimate administrative action, a spike in revocations or unplanned revocations can indicate a response to a detected compromise involving stolen application access tokens.
references:
  - https://www.bleepingcomputer.com/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-06-20
tags:
  - attack.t1528
  - attack.credential_access
level: medium
logsource:
  product: salesforce
  service: audit
detection:
  selection:
    event_type: 'SecurityAlert'
    operation: 'OauthTokenRevoked'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects the revocation of OAuth tokens in Salesforce, which may occur in response to a compromise.
